# Figure description: 1_Геол_карта_M-42-IX.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/M-42-IX/1_Геол_карта_M-42-IX.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **composite geological document** consisting of four sub-figures:
1. A **regional geological map** (1:200,000 scale) — the dominant element
2. Three **geological-geophysical cross-sections** (разрезы) along survey lines GD, IK, and MN, each combining gravity/magnetic anomaly curves with interpreted subsurface geology
3. A small **index/coverage scheme** (схема) in the lower right of the map panel

---

## Coverage and Subject

**Geological map sheet:** М-42-IX (as stated in the title block), covering a portion of northern Kazakhstan at 1:200,000 scale. The map was compiled by the Central Geological-Prospecting Expedition (Центральная геолого-поисковая экспедиция).

**Cross-sections:**
- **Разрез по линии ГД** (Section along line GD) — the longest section, running across the full width of the map sheet, labeled with points Г (left/west) and Д (right/east)
- **Разрез по линии ИК** (Section along line IK) — shorter section in the lower left
- **Разрез по линии МН** (Section along line MN) — shorter section in the lower right, labeled with points М and Н

The index scheme in the upper right identifies this sheet in relation to adjacent sheets M-42 and N-42. Authors listed: **Смольянова Е.М., Филатова Г.В., Куринов В.В., Глусов А.М.**

Year: **1984**

---

## Key Content

### Geological Map
The map displays significant geological complexity, particularly in the **western and central portions**, which are densely colored and patterned, indicating a variety of rock types and ages. Key observations:

- **Western half**: Highly varied geology with interlocking bodies of **orange, pink, salmon, and brown tones**, indicative of intrusive rocks (granites, granitoids) of different ages, likely Paleozoic. Numerous structural boundaries (faults, contacts) are drawn as solid and dashed lines, suggesting a tectonically complex zone.
- **Central zone**: A prominent **white/cream-colored area** (likely Mesozoic or Cenozoic sedimentary cover or platform deposits) dominates the central portion, with relatively few structural markings — suggesting a sedimentary basin or cover sequence with subdued topography.
- **Yellow/ochre patches**: Scattered throughout, likely representing Quaternary or Neogene sediments, alluvial deposits, or specific lithological units.
- **Eastern half**: Predominantly **white/light-colored**, representing younger sedimentary cover with minimal outcrop complexity. River systems (blue lines) are more prominent here, with labeled watercourses.
- **Elevation points**: Numerous spot heights are marked (e.g., **-817, -388, -379, -350, -302, -280**), some appearing as negative values, possibly indicating sub-sea-level depression elevations or borehole reference depths.
- **Structural lines**: Multiple fault systems trending NW-SE and NE-SW are visible across the western portion.
- **Boreholes/drill sites**: Small symbols appear at several locations, consistent with verification drilling.
- Contour interval: **40 metres** (stated in the legend: "Сплошные горизонтали проведены через 40 метров")

### Cross-Sections

**Разрез по линии ГД** (the most detailed):
- Upper portion shows **two geophysical curves**: ΔT, ΔZ (magnetic anomaly, in nT/mT) on the left axis and **Δg (gravity anomaly in mGal)** on the right axis. The magnetic curve shows significant variation — relatively flat in the west, with a sharp **positive spike** (~+1000 nT equivalent) near the eastern portion of the section, and the gravity curve shows moderate undulation.
- Lower portion (geological interpretation) shows layered stratigraphy extending to approximately **-4000 to -5000 m depth**. Multiple colored units are visible:
  - **Pink/red layers**: likely crystalline basement (Precambrian or early Paleozoic)
  - **Green units**: possibly basic/ultrabasic intrusions or specific Paleozoic formations
  - **Gray-blue layered sequences**: sedimentary cover units
  - **Hatched zones**: faulted or uncertain contacts
- Labeled geological units along the section include formation codes consistent with Paleozoic stratigraphy (e.g., references to units like **S₁, O, С₁, T, J** — Silurian, Ordovician, Carboniferous, Triassic, Jurassic)
- Vertical scale reaches approximately **-4000 m** below surface

**Разрез по линии ИК**:
- Shows gravity (Δg) and magnetic (ΔZ) curves in the upper panel with moderate anomalies
- Geological section extends to approximately **-4000 m**
- Includes labeled boreholes/intersection points along the profile

**Разрез по линии МН**:
- Similar format; gravity and magnetic curves shown
- Geological interpretation to ~**-4000–5000 m** depth
- Point М on left (west), Н on right (east)
- Visible unit labels include references to **Z-94, B-М, 2-94** and similar alphanumeric borehole/survey identifiers
